name: vfrenzy
description: Instantly identify VS Code windows by customizing the status bar color and displaying the workspace name.

vfrenzy.statusBarColor.description: Status bar background color (hex, e.g. #007ACC). Leave empty to use default.

vfrenzy.statusBarFontColor.description: Status bar foreground/text color (hex, e.g. #FFFFFF). Leave empty to use default.

vfrenzy.showWorkspaceName.description: Show the workspace name as a badge in the status bar.

vfrenzy.workspaceNameColor.description: Workspace name badge text color (hex, e.g. #FFD700). Leave empty to inherit from status bar.

enumDescriptions: Display workspace name in bold (Unicode bold characters)
vfrenzy.workspaceNameStyle.description: Style for the workspace name badge text.

vfrenzy.workspaceNamePrefix.description: Prefix for the workspace name badge (supports codicons like $(folder), $(star-full)). Ignored when vfrenzy.workspaceNameIcon is set.

vfrenzy.workspaceNameIcon.description: Codicon ID to show before the workspace name (e.g. folder, star-full, rocket, home). When set, overrides the icon portion of workspaceNamePrefix. Leave empty to use workspaceNamePrefix as-is. See https://code.visualstudio.com/api/references/icons-in-labels for available icons.

vfrenzy.customName.description: Custom display name shown in the status bar badge instead of the workspace name. Leave empty to use the workspace name.

vfrenzy.workspaceNameBadgeDecoration.description: Decorative pattern applied around the workspace name badge text. Note: VS Code's status bar background only supports solid hex colors via the official API; true CSS background patterns (diagonal stripes, gradients) are not possible without a CSS-injection extension. These decorations enhance the text area itself using Unicode characters.

vfrenzy.shellCommands.description: Named shell commands that can be opened as terminal tabs via VFrenzy.

name.description: Display name for this shell command (shown in the tab title and quick pick).

command.description: Shell command to execute outside a dev-container (or when no dev-container override is set).

devContainerCommand.description: Shell command to execute when running inside a dev-container. Overrides 'command' in that context.

vfrenzy.shellCommandConfirm.description: Show a confirmation dialog with the command text before opening a shell tab. Set to false to run immediately.
